# pylywsdxx

This is a Python3 library to allow interrogation of Xiaomi Mijia LYWSD* sensors via Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE).

**Note**: This is an unofficial project, and is in no way supported or endorsed by Xiaomi.

## Requirements

This module requires [`bluepy3`](https://pypi.org/project/bluepy3/) which should be installed automagically when using the installation instructions below.

Development of this package is done in Python 3.11. The package is considered forwards compatible at least upto Python 3.12 and probably also beyond. Backwards compatibility is not guaranteed; if it works on Python 3.9 or before consider yourself lucky. [Python versions that are end-of-life](https://devguide.python.org/versions/) are not supported.

The package has been extensively tested on a Raspberry Pi 3 Model B+ (aarch64) with Debian GNU Linux 12 /w Python 3.11.*.


## Installation

```
pip install pylywsdxx
```

## Usage

```
import pylywsdxx as pyly

mac = "A4:C1:38:0D:EA:D0"

device2 = pyly.Lywsd02(mac)
data2 = device2.data
print(f"Temperature: {data2.temperature}˚C")

device3 = pyly.Lywsd03(mac)
data3 = device3.data
print(f"Temperature: {data3.temperature}˚C")
```

Please note that this module has completely and intentionally broken backwards compatibility with previous
and existing versions of `lywsd02` and `lywsd03mmc` and with v1.* versions of itself.

## Acknowledgements

Based on previous work stolen from Mikhail Baranov (`h4`) : [lywsd02](https://github.com/h4/lywsd02)
and Duncan Barclay (`uduncanu`) : [lywsd03mmc](https://github.com/uduncanu/lywsd03mmc)


## Disclaimer & License
As of September 2024 `pylywsdxx` is distributed under [AGPL-3.0-or-later](LICENSE).

methods.name: pylywsdxx.manager.PyLyManager.__init__
documentation: Initialise the manager.

methods.name: pylywsdxx.manager.PyLyManager.__enter__
documentation:
future: Support for context management.
Do stuff to start up the manager.

methods.name: pylywsdxx.manager.PyLyManager.__exit__
documentation:
future: Support for context management.
Do stuff to clean up after using the manager.

methods.name: pylywsdxx.manager.PyLyManager.subscribe_to
documentation:
Let the manager subscribe to a device.

Args:
    mac (str): MAC address of the device
    dev_id (str): Give the device a unique id. This id is used later to refer to the device.
    version (int): If not 3, it is assumed that you want to subscribe to a LYWSD02 device.

Returns:
    Nothing.

methods.name: pylywsdxx.manager.PyLyManager.get_state_of
documentation:
Return the last known state of the given device.

Args:
    dev_id (str): id of the device being requested

Returns:
    dict containing state information

methods.name: pylywsdxx.manager.PyLyManager.update
documentation:
Update the device's state information.

Args:
    dev_id: id of the device being updated

Returns:
    nothing. Device info is updated internally.

methods.name: pylywsdxx.manager.PyLyManager.update_all
documentation: Update the state of all devices known to the manager.

methods.name: pylywsdxx.manager.PyLyManager.qos_device
documentation: Determine the device's Quality of Service.

methods.name: pylywsdxx.manager.PyLyManager.handle_fails
documentation:
Handle failing devices.
Log a warning when devices have failed to provide data.
Reset the BT-radio if multiple devices (more than 50%) have failed.

class_variables.name: pylywsdxx.manager.PyLyManager.__HOLD_DURATION
documentation: seconds

Class to manage multiple LYWSD03MMC devices.

* subscribe to device by MAC
* periodically get data from all devices subscribed to
* mitigate device errors and take countermeasures centrally

variables.name: pylywsdxx.device.UUID_UNITS
documentation: _       0x00 - F, 0x01 - C    READ WRITE

variables.name: pylywsdxx.device.UUID_HISTORY
documentation: _     Last idx 152          READ NOTIFY

variables.name: pylywsdxx.device.UUID_HISTORY_3
documentation: _   Last idx 152          READ NOTIFY

variables.name: pylywsdxx.device.UUID_TIME
documentation: _        5 or 4 bytes          READ WRITE

variables.name: pylywsdxx.device.UUID_DATA
documentation: _        3 bytes               READ NOTIFY

variables.name: pylywsdxx.device.UUID_BATTERY
documentation: _     1 byte                READ

variables.name: pylywsdxx.device.UUID_NUM_RECORDS
documentation: _ 8 bytes               READ

variables.name: pylywsdxx.device.UUID_RECORD_IDX
documentation: _  4 bytes               READ WRITE

documentation: Class for connection errors from upstream.

classes.name: pylywsdxx.device.PyLyValueError
documentation: Class for value errors.

classes.name: pylywsdxx.device.SensorData
documentation:
Class to store sensor data.
For LYWSD02 devices temperature and humidity readings are available.
For LYWSD03MMC devices also battery information is available.

classes.name: pylywsdxx.device.Lywsd02

methods.name: pylywsdxx.device.Lywsd02.__init__
documentation:
Initialise a LYWSD02 device

Args:
    mac: MAC-adress of the device.
    notification_timeout: number of seconds to wait for a connection to be made.
    reusable: whether an object is reusable or not. Device-objects that are reusable
              allow for more flexible error-handling when failures occur in the
              communication with a device.
    debug: whether to provide debugging info output.

documentation: why can't the name of this method be changed?

documentation: Handle device connecting and disconnecting
Class to communicate with LYWSD02 devices.

methods.name: pylywsdxx.device.Lywsd03._process_sensor_data
documentation:
Process the sensor data.

Args:
    data (struct): struct containing sensor data

Returns:
    None

methods.name: pylywsdxx.device.Lywsd03.battery
documentation:
Battery percentage is calculated from voltage which comes along with the
temperature and humidity data, so we'll just get it from there.

Returns:
     guestimate of battery percentage

methods.name: pylywsdxx.device.Lywsd03.start_time
documentation:
Work out the start time of the device.
This is done by taking the current time, subtracting the time
taken from the device (the run time), and adding the timezone offset.

Returns:
    datetime: the start time of the device

methods.name: pylywsdxx.device.Lywsd03.time
documentation:
Fetch datetime and timezone of a LYWSD03MMC device

Returns:
   Device's current datetime and timezone

methods.name: pylywsdxx.device.Lywsd03.time
documentation:
Dummy to disable setting the time and timezone.
LYWSD03MMCs don't have visible clocks.

Args:
    dt (datetime): Does nothing

Returns:
    Nothing

methods.name: pylywsdxx.device.Lywsd03.tz_offset
documentation:
Disable setting the time and timezone.
LYWSD03MMCs don't have visible clocks.

Returns:
    Nothing
Class to communicate with LYWSD03MMC devices.
